audit.current_comsol_topology: entrance plate plus rods plus a 5.8 mm exit enclosure inside a finite vacuum block; no continuous grounded side wall over the rod region
audit.current_simion_topology: generated from the same contract and likewise has no continuous grounded side wall over the rod region

derivation.inner_radius_mm_sweep: rod_outer_extent multiplied by each frozen diagnostic ratio
derivation.selection_rule: Select the smallest mechanically feasible radius whose RF harmonics, N=100 transport and exit phase space meet the frozen candidate budgets; oa accelerator size is not an input.

end_closure.entrance: grounded end plate with the existing on-axis entrance aperture
end_closure.exit: grounded end plate with a centered ion aperture; the final aperture and any shape transition belong to the RF-to-oa interface contract
end_closure.rf_feedthroughs: use shielded feedthrough penetrations; no unmodeled open slot may receive a complete-shield claim

two_dimensional_field_screen.purpose: Select at most two cylindrical radii before any three-dimensional transport run.
two_dimensional_field_screen.largest_radius_role: finite-radius convergence reference, not a formal baseline
two_dimensional_field_screen.selection_rule: Retain the smallest radius whose quadrupole coefficient and normalized higher multipoles are numerically stable against the next larger radius; retain one neighbouring radius for N=100 confirmation.
two_dimensional_field_screen.claim_limit: Rod-midpoint 2D field screening only; no entrance, exit, feedthrough, transport or connector claim.

two_dimensional_screen_evidence.conclusion: The smallest radius is indistinguishable from larger radii at the rod midpoint within the measured mesh sensitivity. Retain it and one neighbour for 3D fringe-field and N=100 validation; do not select a physical shield yet.

three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.geometry_scope: RF entrance plate, four full-length rods, continuous coaxial grounded side shield and RF exit grounded front plate; interior vacuum only
local_mesh_scope: exit-fringe diagnostic cylinder r<=3.6 mm and 81.4<=z<=90.2 mm
exit aperture edge contained inside that volume
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.coarse_mesh_role: screening only; hmax=0.5 mm is not a convergence claim
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.partition_rationale: The physical work radius remains r0=4.0 mm. The numerical partition stops at the frozen diagnostic edge r=3.6 mm: an r=4.0 mm partition is exactly tangent to the rod tips, while r=3.9 mm leaves a 0.1 mm artificial narrow region below the coarse local size. No physical aperture or acceptance limit is changed.
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.evaluation_inset_rationale: Nominal samples on the exit-plate faces or r=3.6 mm aperture wall are evaluated 0.001 mm toward the vacuum and reported with both nominal and actual coordinates so COMSOL cannot assign them to an unsolved conductor domain.
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.convergence_rule: Before N=100, compare hmax=0.5 and 0.25 mm for the smallest radius. The field and harmonic metrics must satisfy an explicitly frozen functional budget; otherwise refine again.
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.revised_convergence_rule_after_n100_fail: The local hmax pair changed five particle classifications, and auto 4 versus 3 retained transmission but not phase space. Hold local hmax at 0.5 mm and perform one final auto 2 field-only check; if auto 3 versus 2 is unstable, replace central isotropic tetrahedra with a swept or anisotropic rod-region mesh.
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.radius_selection_rule: After spatial convergence is established, retain the smallest radius whose three-dimensional central and fringe-field metrics remain within the frozen functional budget relative to the neighbouring radius.
three_dimensional_fringe_field_screen.claim_limit: Candidate unit-field comparison only. It does not validate particle transport, oa back-leakage, feedthrough shielding, gas containment or a physical wall thickness.

three_dimensional_screen_evidence.interpretation: The radius effect in the fringe-region core is smaller than the current mesh sensitivity. The largest mesh difference is boundary-adjacent to an ideal aperture edge and cannot be used alone as a functional rejection. Resolve relevance with paired N=100 transport before radius selection.

n100_transport_screen.source: existing frozen interface-readiness particle table and seed
n100_transport_screen.comparison: The initial local hmax=0.5 versus 0.25 mm pair failed. The next authorized pair holds local hmax=0.5 mm and compares global auto 4 versus 3 without changing source, RF waveform, time step or handoff plane. Radius comparison remains blocked.
required_outputs: particle identity and first-forward handoff event
exit transverse velocity and angular distribution
n100_transport_screen.claim_limit: N=100 is authorized only as a paired mesh-sensitivity diagnostic. It may not select a shield radius, validate the RF-to-oa connection or promote a Formal asset.

n100_mesh_sensitivity_evidence.next_allowed_action: Run auto 2 field-only with local hmax=0.5 mm. No auto 2 particles are authorized until the auto 3 to 2 field comparison is interpreted.

global_background_field_evidence.decision: Run paired N=100 at global auto 4 and 3 with local hmax=0.5 mm. No radius selection is authorized.

evidence: 2D midpoint field at 19.776 mm is indistinguishable from 26.368 mm within measured mesh sensitivity
3D radius sensitivity in the fringe core is smaller than the available mesh sensitivity
the full-device hybrid-mesh N=100 pair at 19.776 mm preserved 100/100 RF exit classification and passed the projected shared-clock pulse function gate
s1_candidate_radius_decision.decision_rule: Retain the smallest tested radius because the next larger radius does not change the current functional decision; do not interpret this as a mechanical wall-thickness, feedthrough, gas-containment or Formal selection.

electrical_contract.common_reference: RF entrance plate, cylindrical housing and RF exit enclosure share 0 V; the later RF-to-oa interface must preserve that common reference

rf_component: continuous grounded housing around the RF rods
s1_direct_interface: nominal zero-gap field connection between the centered RF exit aperture and the parameterized oa shield opening
no requirement that RF and oa outer shield dimensions or cross-section shapes match

claim_limit: The 19.776 mm inner radius is retained only for S1 candidate field construction. No physical wall thickness, feedthrough, gas-containment or Formal shield is selected, and no physical RF-to-oa connection is yet proven.
